1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Skill Graph Query Handler
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // MCP tool handler for skill_graph_query — traverses skill graph
5: // relationships from the SQLite-backed store.
6: 
7: import type { SkillFamily } from '../../lib/skill-graph/skill-graph-db.js';
8: import * as skillGraphQueries from '../../lib/skill-graph/skill-graph-queries.js';
9: import { errorResponse, okResponse as envelopeOkResponse } from './response-envelope.js';
10: 
11: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
12: // 1. TYPES
13: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
14: 
15: export type QueryType =
16:   | 'depends_on'
17:   | 'dependents'
18:   | 'enhances'
19:   | 'enhanced_by'
20:   | 'family_members'
21:   | 'conflicts'
22:   | 'transitive_path'
23:   | 'hub_skills'
24:   | 'orphans'
25:   | 'subgraph';
26: 
27: export interface QueryArgs {
28:   queryType: QueryType;
29:   skillId?: string;
30:   sourceSkillId?: string;
31:   targetSkillId?: string;
32:   family?: string;
33:   minInbound?: number;
34:   depth?: number;
35:   limit?: number;
36: }
37: 
38: type HandlerResponse = { content: Array<{ type: string; text: string }> };
39: const SKILL_FAMILIES = new Set<SkillFamily>(['cli', 'mcp', 'sk-code', 'deep-loop', 'sk-util', 'system']);
40: 
41: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
42: // 2. HANDLER
43: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
44: 
45: /** Handle skill_graph_query tool call */
46: export async function handleSkillGraphQuery(
47:   args: QueryArgs,
48: ): Promise<HandlerResponse> {
49:   try {
50:     const limit = clamp(args.limit, 1, 200, 50);
51:     const depth = clamp(args.depth, 1, 10, 2);
52:     const minInbound = clamp(args.minInbound, 0, 200, 2);
53: 
54:     switch (args.queryType) {
55:       case 'depends_on':
56:         return requireSkillId(args.skillId, args.queryType, (skillId) => okResponse({
57:           queryType: args.queryType,
58:           skillId,
59:           relationships: sliceResults(skillGraphQueries.dependsOn(skillId), limit),
60:         }));
61: 
62:       case 'dependents':
63:         return requireSkillId(args.skillId, args.queryType, (skillId) => okResponse({
64:           queryType: args.queryType,
65:           skillId,
66:           relationships: sliceResults(skillGraphQueries.dependents(skillId), limit),
67:         }));
68: 
69:       case 'enhances':
70:         return requireSkillId(args.skillId, args.queryType, (skillId) => okResponse({
71:           queryType: args.queryType,
72:           skillId,
73:           relationships: sliceResults(skillGraphQueries.enhances(skillId), limit),
74:         }));
75: 
76:       case 'enhanced_by':
77:         return requireSkillId(args.skillId, args.queryType, (skillId) => okResponse({
78:           queryType: args.queryType,
79:           skillId,
80:           relationships: sliceResults(skillGraphQueries.enhancedBy(skillId), limit),
81:         }));
82: 
83:       case 'family_members':
84:         if (!args.family || args.family.trim().length === 0) {
85:           return errorResponse('family is required for family_members query');
86:         }
87:         if (!isSkillFamily(args.family)) {
88:           return errorResponse(`family must be one of: ${[...SKILL_FAMILIES].join(', ')}`);
89:         }
90:         return okResponse({
91:           queryType: args.queryType,
92:           family: args.family,
93:           members: sliceResults(skillGraphQueries.familyMembers(args.family), limit),
94:         });
95: 
96:       case 'conflicts':
97:         return requireSkillId(args.skillId, args.queryType, (skillId) => okResponse({
98:           queryType: args.queryType,
99:           skillId,
100:           relationships: sliceResults(skillGraphQueries.conflicts(skillId), limit),
101:         }));
102: 
103:       case 'transitive_path':
104:         if (!args.sourceSkillId || args.sourceSkillId.trim().length === 0) {
105:           return errorResponse('sourceSkillId is required for transitive_path query');
106:         }
107:         if (!args.targetSkillId || args.targetSkillId.trim().length === 0) {
108:           return errorResponse('targetSkillId is required for transitive_path query');
109:         }
110:         return okResponse({
111:           queryType: args.queryType,
112:           sourceSkillId: args.sourceSkillId,
113:           targetSkillId: args.targetSkillId,
114:           path: skillGraphQueries.transitivePath(args.sourceSkillId, args.targetSkillId),
115:         });
116: 
117:       case 'hub_skills':
118:         return okResponse({
119:           queryType: args.queryType,
120:           minInbound,
121:           skills: sliceResults(skillGraphQueries.hubSkills(minInbound), limit),
122:         });
123: 
124:       case 'orphans':
125:         return okResponse({
126:           queryType: args.queryType,
127:           skills: sliceResults(skillGraphQueries.orphans(), limit),
128:         });
129: 
130:       case 'subgraph':
131:         return requireSkillId(args.skillId, args.queryType, (skillId) => okResponse({
132:           queryType: args.queryType,
133:           skillId,
134:           depth,
135:           graph: skillGraphQueries.subgraph(skillId, depth),
136:         }));
137: 
138:       default:
139:         return errorResponse(
140:           `Unknown queryType: "${args.queryType}". Valid types: depends_on, dependents, enhances, enhanced_by, family_members, conflicts, transitive_path, hub_skills, orphans, subgraph`,
141:         );
142:     }
143:   } catch (err: unknown) {
144:     return errorResponse(
145:       `Skill graph query failed: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`,
146:     );
147:   }
148: }
149: 
150: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
151: // 3. HELPERS
152: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
153: 
154: function requireSkillId(
155:   skillId: string | undefined,
156:   queryType: QueryType,
157:   next: (resolvedSkillId: string) => HandlerResponse,
158: ): HandlerResponse {
159:   if (!skillId || skillId.trim().length === 0) {
160:     return errorResponse(`skillId is required for ${queryType} query`);
161:   }
162: 
163:   return next(skillId);
164: }
165: 
166: function isSkillFamily(value: string): value is SkillFamily {
167:   return SKILL_FAMILIES.has(value as SkillFamily);
168: }
169: 
170: function clamp(
171:   value: number | undefined,
172:   minimum: number,
173:   maximum: number,
174:   fallback: number,
175: ): number {
176:   if (typeof value !== 'number' || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
177:     return fallback;
178:   }
179: 
180:   return Math.min(Math.max(value, minimum), maximum);
181: }
182: 
183: function sliceResults<T>(results: T, limit: number): T {
184:   if (!Array.isArray(results)) return results;
185:   return results.slice(0, limit) as T;
186: }
187: 
188: function sanitizeQueryOutput(value: unknown): unknown {
189:   if (Array.isArray(value)) {
190:     return value.map(sanitizeQueryOutput);
191:   }
192: 
193:   if (value && typeof value === 'object') {
194:     const sanitized: Record<string, unknown> = {};
195:     for (const [key, nestedValue] of Object.entries(value)) {
196:       if (key === 'sourcePath' || key === 'contentHash') {
197:         continue;
198:       }
199:       sanitized[key] = sanitizeQueryOutput(nestedValue);
200:     }
201:     return sanitized;
202:   }
203: 
204:   return value;
205: }
206: 
207: function okResponse(data: Record<string, unknown>): HandlerResponse {
208:   return envelopeOkResponse(sanitizeQueryOutput(data) as Record<string, unknown>);
209: }

(End of file - total 209 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Cross-Skill Edges Apply Patch
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Idempotent JSON patching for applying enhance edges to graph-metadata.json.
5: 
6: import { readFile, writeFile } from 'node:fs/promises';
7: import { basename, resolve, sep } from 'node:path';
8: import type { EdgeSourceKind, EdgeWriteIntent, InboundEnhanceCandidate } from './types.js';
9: 
10: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
11: // 1. APPLY EDGE
12: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
13: 
14: const PROTECTED_SOURCE_KINDS = new Set<EdgeSourceKind>(['manual', 'trusted']);
15: 
16: function deriveSourceKind(writeIntent: EdgeWriteIntent): EdgeSourceKind {
17:   return writeIntent === 'trusted-maintainer' ? 'trusted' : 'automated';
18: }
19: 
20: function readSourceKind(edge: Record<string, unknown>): EdgeSourceKind | null {
21:   const value = edge.source_kind;
22:   if (value === 'automated' || value === 'manual' || value === 'trusted') {
23:     return value;
24:   }
25:   return null;
26: }
27: 
28: function buildAutomatedReason(candidate: InboundEnhanceCandidate): string {
29:   return candidate.rules.map(r => `${r.rule}:${r.contribution.toFixed(2)}`).join(' + ');
30: }
31: 
32: /**
33:  * Validate that the source path is a graph-metadata.json file under the trusted skills root.
34:  * Prevents path traversal in apply mode.
35:  */
36: function isPathWithin(candidatePath: string, trustedRoot: string): boolean {
37:   const resolvedCandidate = resolve(candidatePath);
38:   const resolvedRoot = resolve(trustedRoot);
39:   const rootWithSep = resolvedRoot.endsWith(sep) ? resolvedRoot : resolvedRoot + sep;
40:   return resolvedCandidate.startsWith(rootWithSep) && basename(resolvedCandidate) === 'graph-metadata.json';
41: }
42: 
43: /**
44:  * Apply an enhance edge to the source skill's graph-metadata.json.
45:  * Idempotent for automated writes and protects manually maintained provenance.
46:  *
47:  * @param candidate - The candidate to apply
48:  * @param skillsRoot - Optional trusted workspace root. When provided, candidate.sourcePath
49:  *                    MUST resolve under this root and have basename 'graph-metadata.json'.
50:  * This guards against path traversal in apply mode.
51:  */
52: export async function applyEnhanceEdge(
53:   candidate: InboundEnhanceCandidate,
54:   skillsRoot?: string,
55:   writeIntent: EdgeWriteIntent = 'automated',
56: ): Promise<{ applied: boolean; reason: string }> {
57:   if (!candidate.applyable) {
58:     return { applied: false, reason: `not applyable: ${candidate.blockers.join('; ')}` };
59:   }
60: 
61:   if (skillsRoot && !isPathWithin(candidate.sourcePath, skillsRoot)) {
62:     return {
63:       applied: false,
64:       reason: `path-boundary violation: ${candidate.sourcePath} not under ${skillsRoot}`,
65:     };
66:   }
67: 
68:   try {
69:     const raw = await readFile(candidate.sourcePath, 'utf-8');
70:     const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
71: 
72:     if (!parsed.edges) {
73:       parsed.edges = {};
74:     }
75:     if (!parsed.edges.enhances) {
76:       parsed.edges.enhances = [];
77:     }
78: 
79:     const enhances: Array<Record<string, unknown>> = parsed.edges.enhances;
80:     const sourceKind = deriveSourceKind(writeIntent);
81:     const existingEdge = enhances.find(e => e.target === candidate.targetSkillId);
82: 
83:     if (existingEdge && writeIntent === 'automated') {
84:       const existingSourceKind = readSourceKind(existingEdge);
85:       if (existingSourceKind && PROTECTED_SOURCE_KINDS.has(existingSourceKind)) {
86:         return { applied: false, reason: 'edge already exists: manual provenance protected' };
87:       }
88:       return { applied: false, reason: 'edge already exists' };
89:     }
90: 
91:     if (existingEdge) {
92:       existingEdge.weight = candidate.weight;
93:       existingEdge.context = candidate.context;
94:       existingEdge.source_kind = sourceKind;
95:       // A trusted overwrite supersedes any earlier automated origin; drop the
96:       // automated-provenance markers so the edge cannot carry contradictory
97:       // {source_kind:'trusted', auto_added_reason:...} provenance.
98:       delete existingEdge.auto_added_at;
99:       delete existingEdge.auto_added_reason;
100:       await writeFile(candidate.sourcePath, JSON.stringify(parsed, null, 2) + '\n', 'utf-8');
101:       return { applied: true, reason: 'edge updated by trusted maintainer' };
102:     }
103: 
104:     const newEdge = {
105:       target: candidate.targetSkillId,
106:       weight: candidate.weight,
107:       context: candidate.context,
108:       source_kind: sourceKind,
109:     };
110: 
111:     if (writeIntent === 'automated') {
112:       Object.assign(newEdge, {
113:         auto_added_at: new Date().toISOString(),
114:         auto_added_reason: buildAutomatedReason(candidate),
115:       });
116:     }
117: 
118:     enhances.push(newEdge);
119:     parsed.edges.enhances = enhances;
120: 
121:     await writeFile(candidate.sourcePath, JSON.stringify(parsed, null, 2) + '\n', 'utf-8');
122:     return { applied: true, reason: `edge added with ${sourceKind} provenance` };
123:   } catch (error) {
124:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
125:     return { applied: false, reason: `failed to apply edge: ${message}` };
126:   }
127: }

(End of file - total 127 lines)
</content>

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Skill Graph Database
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // SQLite storage for skill graph metadata (nodes + edges).
5: // Uses the advisor package-local skill-graph.sqlite runtime database.
6: 
7: import Database from 'better-sqlite3';
8: // NOTE: lib/shared/embeddings is a symlink to system-spec-kit/shared/embeddings.
9: // The symlink in the file tree makes the cross-skill dependency on
10: // system-spec-kit visible to repo users. The import here uses the @spec-kit/shared workspace
11: // alias (resolved via tsconfig paths) for clean tsc resolution. If system-spec-kit is
12: // deleted, both the symlink and the alias dangle and embeddings-backed features break — the
13: // dangling symlink in the file tree is the documenting failure signal.

15: import { createHash } from 'node:crypto';

17: import { basename, dirname, join, relative, resolve } from 'node:path';
18: import {
19:   ensureVecMetadataTable,
20:   ensureVecTableForDim,
21:   getActiveEmbedder,
22:   hasActiveEmbedderPointer,
23:   vecTableNameForDim,
24: } from '../embedders/schema.js';

26: import type { EmbedderAdapter } from '../embedders/adapter.js';

28: import { parseSkillFrontmatter } from '../utils/skill-markdown.js';

35: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

121: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
122: // 2. CONSTANTS
123: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
124: 
125: export const SCHEMA_VERSION = 1;
126: 
127: export const DB_FILENAME = 'skill-graph.sqlite';
128: 
129: const SKILL_METADATA_FILENAME = 'graph-metadata.json';
130: const MIN_WEIGHT = 0.0;
131: const MAX_WEIGHT = 1.0;
132: 
133: const ALLOWED_FAMILIES: readonly SkillFamily[] = [
134:   'cli',
135:   'mcp',
136:   'sk-code',
137:   'deep-loop',
138:   'sk-util',
139:   'system',
140: ];
141: 
142: const EDGE_TYPES: readonly SkillEdgeType[] = [
143:   'depends_on',
144:   'enhances',
145:   'siblings',
146:   'conflicts_with',
147:   'prerequisite_for',
148: ];
149: 
150: const WEIGHT_BANDS: Readonly<Record<SkillEdgeType, readonly [number, number]>> = {
151:   depends_on: [0.7, 1.0],
152:   prerequisite_for: [0.7, 1.0],
153:   enhances: [0.3, 0.7],
154:   siblings: [0.4, 0.6],
155:   conflicts_with: [0.5, 1.0],
156: } as const;
157: 
158: function isWalFallbackError(error: unknown): boolean {
159:   const code = (error as { code?: string }).code ?? '';
160:   // better-sqlite3/libsqlite3 on SQLite 3.x may surface base or extended result codes.
161:   return /^(?:SQLITE_READONLY|SQLITE_CANTOPEN|SQLITE_IOERR)(?:_|$)/.test(code)
162:     || code === 'EACCES'
163:     || code === 'EROFS'
164:     || code === 'EPERM'
165:     || code === 'ENOSPC';
166: }
167: 
168: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
169: // 3. SCHEMA
170: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
171: 
172: const SCHEMA_SQL = `
173:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS skill_nodes (
174:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
175:     family TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(family IN ('cli', 'mcp', 'sk-code', 'deep-loop', 'sk-util', 'system')),
176:     category TEXT NOT NULL,
177:     schema_version INTEGER NOT NULL,
178:     domains TEXT,
179:     intent_signals TEXT,
180:     derived TEXT,
181:     source_path TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
182:     content_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
183:     embedding BLOB,
184:     embedding_model_id TEXT,
185:     embedding_content_hash TEXT,
186:     indexed_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
187:   );
188: 
189:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS skill_edges (
190:     id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
191:     source_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES skill_nodes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
192:     target_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES skill_nodes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
193:     edge_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(edge_type IN ('depends_on', 'enhances', 'siblings', 'conflicts_with', 'prerequisite_for')),
194:     weight REAL NOT NULL CHECK(weight >= 0.0 AND weight <= 1.0),
195:     context TEXT NOT NULL,
196:     UNIQUE(source_id, target_id, edge_type),
197:     CHECK(source_id != target_id)
198:   );
199: 
200:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version (
201:     version INTEGER NOT NULL
202:   );
203: 
204:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS skill_graph_metadata (
205:     key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
206:     value TEXT,
207:     updated_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
208:   );
209: 
210:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS vec_metadata (
211:     key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
212:     value TEXT NOT NULL,
213:     created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
214:   );
215: 
216:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS skill_docs (
217:     id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
218:     skill_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES skill_nodes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
219:     doc_path TEXT NOT NULL,
220:     title TEXT,
221:     description TEXT,
222:     trigger_phrases TEXT NOT NULL,
223:     importance_tier TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'normal',
224:     context_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'general',
225:     content_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
226:     indexed_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
227:     UNIQUE(skill_id, doc_path)
228:   );
229: 
230:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_skill_docs_skill ON skill_docs(skill_id);
231: 
232:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_skill_nodes_family ON skill_nodes(family);
233:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_skill_nodes_category ON skill_nodes(category);
234:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_skill_nodes_hash ON skill_nodes(content_hash);
235:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_skill_edges_source ON skill_edges(source_id, edge_type);
236:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_skill_edges_target ON skill_edges(target_id, edge_type);
237:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_skill_edges_type ON skill_edges(edge_type);
238: `;
239: 
240: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
241: // 4. DATABASE LIFECYCLE
242: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
243: 
244: let db: Database.Database | null = null;
245: let dbPath: string | null = null;
246: let readOnlyDb: Database.Database | null = null;
247: 
248: // The env override is read on every call (never captured at module load) so a
249: // diagnostic probe and the writing daemon always agree on the artifact path
250: // even if the override is set after either module first loaded. `baseRoot`
251: // defaults to the writer's cwd; a caller resolving for a specific workspace
252: // (e.g. the status probe) passes that root so both sides target one file.
253: export function resolveSkillGraphDbDir(baseRoot: string = process.cwd()): string {
254:   const overrideDbDir = process.env.MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_DB_DIR ?? process.env.SYSTEM_SKILL_ADVISOR_DB_DIR;
255:   if (overrideDbDir) {
256:     return resolve(overrideDbDir);
257:   }
258:   return resolve(
259:     baseRoot,
260:     '.opencode',
261:     'skills',
262:     'system-skill-advisor',
263:     'mcp_server',
264:     'database',
265:   );
266: }
267: 
268: export function getSkillGraphDbPath(): string {
269:   return dbPath ?? join(resolveSkillGraphDbDir(), DB_FILENAME);
270: }
271: 
272: const CORRUPTION_REASON_PATTERN = /SQLITE_CORRUPT|SQLITE_NOTADB|database disk image is malformed|file is not a database|malformed/i;
273: const TRANSIENT_REASON_PATTERN = /database is locked|SQLITE_BUSY|SQLITE_LOCKED|SQLITE_PROTOCOL|EIO|EPERM|EACCES|EROFS|ENOSPC/i;
274: 
275: /**
276:  * Only genuine on-disk corruption may trigger destructive recovery.
277:  *
278:  * Transient conditions (lock contention from a concurrent daemon, I/O or
279:  * permission errors) must never rename/delete the live database out from
280:  * under its owner, so they fail this check and initDb proceeds to a normal
281:  * open (busy_timeout handles contention).
282:  */
283: export function isGenuineCorruptionReason(reason: string): boolean {
284:   if (reason.startsWith('SQLITE_CHECK_FAILED') || TRANSIENT_REASON_PATTERN.test(reason)) {
285:     return false;
286:   }
287:   // quick_check completed and reported integrity violations
288:   if (reason.startsWith('SQLITE_QUICK_CHECK_')) {
289:     return true;
290:   }
291:   return CORRUPTION_REASON_PATTERN.test(reason);
292: }
293: 
294: function recoverMalformedDatabase(databasePath: string, reason: string): void {
295:   if (!existsSync(databasePath)) {
296:     return;
297:   }
298: 
299:   const backupPath = join(dirname(databasePath), `${DB_FILENAME}.${Date.now()}.corrupt`);
300:   try {
301:     renameSync(databasePath, backupPath);
302:   } catch {
303:     rmSync(databasePath, { force: true });
304:   }
305:   console.warn(`[skill-graph] Recovered malformed SQLite database via initDb quick_check (${reason})`);
306: }
307: 
308: function ensureSchemaMigrations(database: Database.Database): void {
309:   ensureVecMetadataTable(database);
310:   ensureVecTableForDim(database, 768);
311:   ensureVecTableForDim(database, 1024);
312: 
313:   database.exec(`
314:     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS skill_graph_metadata (
315:       key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
316:       value TEXT,
317:       updated_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
318:     );
319:     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS skill_docs (
320:       id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
321:       skill_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES skill_nodes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
322:       doc_path TEXT NOT NULL,
323:       title TEXT,
324:       description TEXT,
325:       trigger_phrases TEXT NOT NULL,
326:       importance_tier TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'normal',
327:       context_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'general',
328:       content_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
329:       indexed_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
330:       UNIQUE(skill_id, doc_path)
331:     );
332:     CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_skill_docs_skill ON skill_docs(skill_id);
333:     CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_skill_nodes_family ON skill_nodes(family);
334:     CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_skill_edges_source ON skill_edges(source_id, edge_type);
335:     CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_skill_edges_target ON skill_edges(target_id, edge_type);
336:   `);
337: 
338:   const columns = new Set(
339:     (database.prepare('PRAGMA table_info(skill_nodes)').all() as Array<{ name: string }>)
340:       .map((column) => column.name),
341:   );
342:   if (!columns.has('embedding')) {
343:     database.exec('ALTER TABLE skill_nodes ADD COLUMN embedding BLOB');
344:   }
345:   if (!columns.has('embedding_model_id')) {
346:     database.exec('ALTER TABLE skill_nodes ADD COLUMN embedding_model_id TEXT');
347:   }
348:   if (!columns.has('embedding_content_hash')) {
349:     database.exec('ALTER TABLE skill_nodes ADD COLUMN embedding_content_hash TEXT');
350:   }
351:   database.exec(`
352:     CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_skill_nodes_embedding_model ON skill_nodes(embedding_model_id);
353:     CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_skill_nodes_embedding_hash ON skill_nodes(embedding_content_hash);
354:   `);
355: }
356: 
357: /** Initialize (or get) the skill graph database. */
358: export function initDb(dbDir: string): Database.Database {
359:   if (db) return db;
360: 
361:   try {
362:     mkdirSync(dbDir, { recursive: true });
363:     dbPath = join(dbDir, DB_FILENAME);
364:     const integrity = checkSqliteIntegrity(dbPath);
365:     if (!integrity.ok && integrity.reason !== 'SQLITE_ABSENT' && isGenuineCorruptionReason(integrity.reason)) {
366:       recoverMalformedDatabase(dbPath, integrity.reason);
367:     }
368:     db = new Database(dbPath);
369:     db.pragma('busy_timeout = 5000');
370:     try {
371:       db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL');
372:     } catch (error: unknown) {
373:       const code = (error as { code?: string }).code ?? '';
374:       if (isWalFallbackError(error)) {
375:         console.warn(`[skill-graph] WAL mode unavailable (${code}); falling back to journal_mode=DELETE. Concurrent readers may stall during writes; performance degraded vs WAL mode.`);
376:         db.pragma('journal_mode = DELETE');
377:       } else {
378:         throw error;
379:       }
380:     }
381:     db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON');
382:     db.exec(SCHEMA_SQL);
383:     ensureSchemaMigrations(db);
384: 
385:     const versionRow = db.prepare('SELECT version FROM schema_version LIMIT 1').get() as { version: number } | undefined;
386:     if (!versionRow) {
387:       db.prepare('INSERT INTO schema_version (version) VALUES (?)').run(SCHEMA_VERSION);
388:     } else if (versionRow.version < SCHEMA_VERSION) {
389:       db.prepare('UPDATE schema_version SET version = ?').run(SCHEMA_VERSION);
390:     }
391: 
392:     return db;
393:   } catch (error) {
394:     if (db) {
395:       try { db.close(); } catch { /* best effort cleanup for failed init */ }
396:     }
397:     db = null;
398:     dbPath = null;
399:     throw error;
400:   }
401: }
402: 
403: /** Get the current database instance (lazy-initializes if needed). */
404: export function getDb(): Database.Database {
405:   if (!db) initDb(resolveSkillGraphDbDir());
406:   return db!;
407: }
408: 
409: /**
410:  * Read-only corruption probe for status surfaces. Returns null when this
411:  * process already owns a live read-write handle: the on-disk database is
412:  * healthy by construction, because initDb would have recovered any genuine
413:  * corruption before that handle opened. Otherwise probes the file with
414:  * quick_check WITHOUT opening the read-write handle or running destructive
415:  * recovery, so a read-only status call can REPORT corruption rather than
416:  * silently quarantining the database as a side effect of being asked for
417:  * counts.
418:  */
419: export function probeStatusIntegrity(): { ok: true } | { ok: false; reason: string } | null {
420:   if (db) return null;
421:   return checkSqliteIntegrity(getSkillGraphDbPath());
422: }
423: 
424: /**
425:  * Read-only database access for recommend-path consumers (semantic shadow
426:  * lane). Never creates the database file, never runs schema migrations, and
427:  * never triggers malformed-database recovery, so secondary/bridge processes
428:  * cannot become a second writer on the daemon-owned skill graph. Reuses the
429:  * read-write handle when this process already owns one (daemon path).
430:  * Returns null when the database file is absent or unopenable; callers must
431:  * degrade gracefully (no shadow scoring).
432:  */
433: export function getDbReadOnly(): Database.Database | null {
434:   if (db) return db;
435:   if (readOnlyDb) return readOnlyDb;
436: 
437:   const databasePath = join(resolveSkillGraphDbDir(), DB_FILENAME);
438:   if (!existsSync(databasePath)) {
439:     return null;
440:   }
441: 
442:   try {
443:     const handle = new Database(databasePath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true });
444:     handle.pragma('busy_timeout = 5000');
445:     readOnlyDb = handle;
446:     return readOnlyDb;
447:   } catch (error: unknown) {
448:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
449:     console.warn(`[skill-graph] Read-only open failed (${message}); shadow scoring degraded for this process`);
450:     return null;
451:   }
452: }
453: 
454: /** Close the database connection. */
455: export function closeDb(): void {
456:   if (db) {
457:     db.close();
458:     db = null;
459:     dbPath = null;
460:   }
461:   if (readOnlyDb) {
462:     try {
463:       readOnlyDb.close();
464:     } catch { /* best effort: read-only handle teardown */ }
465:     readOnlyDb = null;
466:   }
467: }
468: 
469: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
470: // 5. METADATA HELPERS
471: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
472: 
473: function getMetadata(key: string): string | null {
474:   const database = getDb();
475:   const row = database.prepare('SELECT value FROM skill_graph_metadata WHERE key = ?').get(key) as { value: string } | undefined;
476:   return row?.value ?? null;
477: }
478: 
479: function setMetadata(key: string, value: string): void {
480:   const database = getDb();
481:   const now = new Date().toISOString();
482:   database.prepare(`
483:     INSERT INTO skill_graph_metadata (key, value, updated_at)
484:     VALUES (?, ?, ?)
485:     ON CONFLICT(key) DO UPDATE SET value = excluded.value, updated_at = excluded.updated_at
486:   `).run(key, value, now);
487: }
488: 
489: function checkpointWal(database: Database.Database): void {
490:   try {
491:     database.pragma('wal_checkpoint(FULL)');
492:   } catch (error: unknown) {
493:     const code = (error as { code?: string }).code ?? '';
494:     if (!isWalFallbackError(error) && code !== 'SQLITE_BUSY') {
495:       throw error;
496:     }
497:   }
498: }
499: 
500: export function getLastScanTimestamp(): string | null {
501:   return getMetadata('last_scan_timestamp');
502: }
503: 
504: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
505: // 6. INDEXING HELPERS
506: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
507: 
508: function isRecord(value: unknown): value is JsonRecord {
509:   return typeof value === 'object' && value !== null && !Array.isArray(value);
510: }
511: 
512: function toDisplayPath(filePath: string): string {
513:   const relativePath = relative(process.cwd(), filePath);
514:   return relativePath && !relativePath.startsWith('..') ? relativePath : filePath;
515: }
516: 
517: function requireString(value: unknown, fieldName: string, sourcePath: string): string {
518:   if (typeof value !== 'string' || value.trim().length === 0) {
519:     throw new Error(`${toDisplayPath(sourcePath)}: ${fieldName} must be a non-empty string`);
520:   }
521:   return value;
522: }
523: 
524: function requireStringArray(value: unknown, fieldName: string, sourcePath: string): string[] {
525:   if (!Array.isArray(value) || value.some((entry) => typeof entry !== 'string')) {
526:     throw new Error(`${toDisplayPath(sourcePath)}: ${fieldName} must be an array of strings`);
527:   }
528:   return value;
529: }
530: 
531: function requireDerived(value: unknown, schemaVersion: number, sourcePath: string): JsonRecord | null {
532:   if (schemaVersion === 1) {
533:     return isRecord(value) ? value : null;
534:   }
535: 
536:   if (!isRecord(value)) {
537:     throw new Error(`${toDisplayPath(sourcePath)}: schema_version 2 requires a derived object`);
538:   }
539: 
540:   return value;
541: }
542: 
543: function requireEdgeWeight(value: unknown, edgeType: SkillEdgeType, sourcePath: string): number {
544:   if (typeof value !== 'number' || Number.isNaN(value)) {
545:     throw new Error(`${toDisplayPath(sourcePath)}: ${edgeType} edge weight must be a number`);
546:   }
547: 
548:   if (value < MIN_WEIGHT || value > MAX_WEIGHT) {
549:     throw new Error(
550:       `${toDisplayPath(sourcePath)}: ${edgeType} edge weight ${value} out of range [${MIN_WEIGHT}, ${MAX_WEIGHT}]`,
551:     );
552:   }
553: 
554:   return value;
555: }
556: 
557: function warnWeightBand(edge: SkillEdge, sourcePath: string, warnings: string[]): void {
558:   const [minBand, maxBand] = WEIGHT_BANDS[edge.edgeType];
559:   if (edge.weight >= minBand && edge.weight <= maxBand) {
560:     return;
561:   }
562: 
563:   const warning = `WEIGHT-BAND: ${edge.sourceId} ${edge.edgeType} ${edge.targetId} weight ${edge.weight} ` +
564:     `outside recommended band [${minBand}, ${maxBand}] (${toDisplayPath(sourcePath)})`;
565:   warnings.push(warning);
566:   console.warn(`[skill-graph] ${warning}`);
567: }
568: 
569: function computeContentHash(content: string): string {
570:   return createHash('sha256').update(content).digest('hex');
571: }
572: 
573: function encodeEmbedding(vector: Float32Array | readonly number[]): Buffer {
574:   const array = vector instanceof Float32Array ? vector : Float32Array.from(vector);
575:   return Buffer.from(array.buffer, array.byteOffset, array.byteLength);
576: }
577: 
578: function decodeEmbedding(blob: Buffer): Float32Array | null {
579:   if (blob.byteLength === 0 || blob.byteLength % Float32Array.BYTES_PER_ELEMENT !== 0) {
580:     return null;
581:   }
582:   const copied = blob.buffer.slice(blob.byteOffset, blob.byteOffset + blob.byteLength);
583:   return new Float32Array(copied);
584: }
585: 
586: function skillDescriptionForEmbedding(sourcePath: string): string {
587:   const skillMdPath = join(dirname(sourcePath), 'SKILL.md');
588:   if (!existsSync(skillMdPath)) {
589:     return '';
590:   }
591:   const parsed = parseSkillFrontmatter(readFileSync(skillMdPath, 'utf8'));
592:   return parsed.frontmatter.description || '';
593: }
594: 
595: function providerModelId(profile: { provider: string; model: string; dim: number; dtype?: string | null; slug?: string }): string {
596:   return profile.slug ?? [profile.provider, profile.model, profile.dim, profile.dtype ?? null]
597:     .filter((part): part is string | number => part !== null && part !== undefined && part !== '')
598:     .join(':');
599: }
600: 
601: function discoverGraphMetadataFiles(skillDir: string): string[] {
602:   if (!existsSync(skillDir)) {
603:     return [];
604:   }
605: 
606:   const discovered: string[] = [];
607:   const stack: string[] = [skillDir];
608: 
609:   while (stack.length > 0) {
610:     const currentDir = stack.pop()!;
611:     const entries = readdirSync(currentDir, { withFileTypes: true });
612:     for (const entry of entries) {
613:       const entryPath = join(currentDir, entry.name);
614:       if (entry.isDirectory()) {
615:         stack.push(entryPath);
616:         continue;
617:       }
618:

135:   const base = C.baseConstant
136:     + Math.min(1, args.liveNormalized * C.liveNormalizedRampGain) * C.liveNormalizedRampCoefficient;
137:   if (args.readOnlyExplainer && !args.readOnlyRouteAllowed) return C.readOnlyExplainerFloor;
138:   if (args.readOnlyRouteAllowed) {
139:     return Number(Math.max(base, C.readOnlyRouteAllowedFloor).toFixed(4));
140:   }
141:   if (args.derivedDominant && args.directScore < C.derivedDominantDirectScoreCeiling) {
142:     return C.derivedDominantConfidence;

144:   if (args.hasTaskIntent
145:     && (args.directScore >= C.taskIntentDirectScoreFloor
146:       || args.liveNormalized >= C.taskIntentLiveNormalizedFloor)) {

148:     // with task-intent + many weak signals (saturating liveNormalized) but
149:     // no strong direct anchor would force confidence to taskIntentFloor for
150:     // many unrelated skills. The guard fires when liveNormalized is near-
151:     // saturated AND directScore is below the directScoreLiftThreshold —
152:     // the precise signature of a token-stuffed prompt. Legitimate task-intent
153:     // prompts have at least one strong direct hit and pass via directScore;
154:     // they don't hit this branch. When the guard fires we fall through to
155:     // the standard `base + directBonus` math below.
156:     const dispersionGuardTripped = args.liveNormalized >= 0.95
157:       && args.directScore < C.directScoreLiftThreshold;
158:     if (!dispersionGuardTripped) {

162:   if (args.directScore >= C.directScoreLiftThreshold) {
163:     return Number(Math.max(base, C.directScoreFloor).toFixed(4));
164:   }
165:   const directBonus = args.directScore >= C.directScoreBonusThreshold ? C.directScoreBonus : 0;

175:   let uncertainty = U.noEvidenceDefault;

177:   else if (evidenceCount >= U.mediumEvidenceCount) uncertainty = U.mediumFloor;
178:   else if (evidenceCount >= U.someEvidenceCount) uncertainty = U.elevatedFloor;
179:   if (direct >= U.directEvidenceDiscountThreshold) uncertainty -= U.directEvidenceDiscount;
180:   if (confidence < U.lowConfidencePenaltyThreshold) uncertainty += U.lowConfidencePenalty;
181:   uncertainty += ambiguousPressure;

1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: // ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
3: // ║ COMPONENT: Skill Advisor Plugin Bridge (mk-skill-advisor, MJS source)   ║
4: // ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
5: // ║ PURPOSE: Subprocess bridge between `.opencode/plugins/mk-skill-         ║
6: // ║          advisor.js` and the standalone mk_skill_advisor MCP       ║
7: // ║          server. The plugin                                           ║
8: // ║          spawns this script with stdin JSON; this script writes a      ║
9: // ║          single stdout JSON response and exits.                         ║
10: // ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
11: //
12: // This file is the SOURCE-OF-TRUTH bridge. It lives outside the
13: // TypeScript build tree on purpose:
14: //   1. The OpenCode plugin host imports `.opencode/plugins/*.js` only — having
15: //      the bridge as a sibling .mjs keeps the plugin entrypoint count to 1
16: //      while letting the bridge use ESM and dynamic imports without TS
17: //      compile coordination on every plugin reload.
18: //   2. The bridge is a thin subprocess wrapper (parse stdin → call native
19: //      compat handlers → emit stdout JSON) and benefits from being editable
20: //      as plain JS for live diagnosis when the OpenCode plugin host misroutes.
21: //   3. Migrating to TS would require either (a) building this file into the
22: //      same dist tree the plugin watches, creating a chicken-and-egg with
23: //      cache-signature freshness, or (b) maintaining a
24: //      .ts → .mjs build step exclusive to this single file. Either path is
25: //      a larger packet than the source/dist boundary fixes here.
26: //
27: // Smoke tests asserting the subprocess envelope live in the standalone
28: // system-skill-advisor compatibility test suite.
29: // This file intentionally lives outside
30: // `.opencode/plugins/` so OpenCode discovers only real plugin entrypoints.
31:

93:     status: STATUS_VALUES.has(args.status) ? args.status : 'fail_open',

123: async function withStdoutSilenced(fn) {
124:   const forwarded = (...args) => {
125:     const rendered = args
126:       .map((value) => (typeof value === 'string' ? value : JSON.stringify(value)))
127:       .join(' ');
128:     process.stderr.write(`${rendered}\n`);
129:   };
130: 
131:   const original = {
132:     log: console.log,
133:     info: console.info,
134:     debug: console.debug,
135:     warn: console.warn,
136:   };
137: 
138:   console.log = forwarded;
139:   console.info = forwarded;
140:   console.debug = forwarded;
141:   console.warn = forwarded;
142: 
143:   try {
144:     return await fn();
145:   } finally {
146:     console.log = original.log;
147:     console.info = original.info;
148:     console.debug = original.debug;
149:     console.warn = original.warn;
150:   }

153: function parseInput(text) {
154:   if (!text.trim()) {
155:     throw Object.assign(new Error('Missing bridge input'), { code: 'MISSING_INPUT' });
156:   }
157:   const parsed = JSON.parse(text);
158:   if (typeof parsed?.prompt !== 'string') {
159:     throw Object.assign(new Error('Missing prompt'), { code: 'MISSING_PROMPT' });
160:   }
161:   if (typeof parsed?.workspaceRoot !== 'string' || !parsed.workspaceRoot.trim()) {
162:     throw Object.assign(new Error('Missing workspace root'), { code: 'MISSING_WORKSPACE_ROOT' });
163:   }
164:   return parsed;

169: }

172:   if (!Number.isFinite(value)) {
173:     return DEFAULT_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD;
174:   }
175:   return Math.min(1, Math.max(0, Number(value)));
176: }

179:   return Number.isFinite(value) ? Number(value).toFixed(2) : '0.00';
180: }

182: function sanitizeLabel(value) {
183:   if (typeof value !== 'string') {
184:     return null;
185:   }
186:   const cleaned = value.replace(/[\u0000-\u001F\u007F]/g, '').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
187:   if (!cleaned || cleaned.length > 160 || /\b(ignore|override|forget|bypass|disable|execute|run|call|tool|developer|assistant|previous instructions|all instructions)\b/i.test(cleaned) || /<!--|-->|```|<script\b|<\/script>|^\s*(system|instruction|developer|assistant)\s*:/i.test(cleaned)) {
188:     return null;
189:   }

194:   return Object.fromEntries(
195:     Object.entries(sourceEnv).filter((entry) => CHILD_ENV_ALLOWLIST.has(entry[0]) && typeof entry[1] === 'string'),
196:   );
197: }
198: 
199: function resolveCliSocketDir(env = process.env) {
200:   // Default to the same short /tmp directory as the launcher and CLI shim.
201:   // Defaulting to the database directory produced a socket path longer than
202:   // Darwin's 103-byte sun_path limit, so the warm probe always failed and the
203:   // CLI fallback could never engage. An explicit env override still wins.
204:   return env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR ?? DEFAULT_SOCKET_DIR;
205: }
206: 
207: function createCliChildEnv(sourceEnv = process.env) {
208:   return {
209:     ...createChildEnv(sourceEnv),
210:     SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR: resolveCliSocketDir(sourceEnv),
211:     MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_CLI_PROMPT_TIME: '1',
212:   };
213: }
214: 
215: function resolveCliSocketPath(env = process.env) {
216:   const socketDir = resolveCliSocketDir(env);
217:   if (socketDir.startsWith('tcp://')) {
218:     return socketDir;
219:   }
220:   return resolve(socketDir, SOCKET_FILE_NAME);
221: }
222: 
223: function socketPathTooLong(socketPath) {
224:   if (socketPath.startsWith('tcp://')) return false;
225:   return process.platform === 'darwin' && Buffer.byteLength(socketPath) > 103;
226: }
227: 
228: function positiveIntFromEnv(value, fallback) {
229:   if (typeof value !== 'string' || value.trim().length === 0) {
230:     return fallback;
231:   }
232:   const parsed = Number.parseInt(value, 10);
233:   return Number.isFinite(parsed) && parsed > 0 ? parsed : fallback;
234: }
235: 
236: function resolveCliTimeoutMs(env = process.env) {
237:   return positiveIntFromEnv(env.SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_CLI_FALLBACK_TIMEOUT_MS, ADVISOR_CLI_TIMEOUT_MS);
238: }
239: 
240: function loadBridgeHelper() {
241:   try {
242:     const bridge = require(ADVISOR_BRIDGE_HELPER_PATH);
243:     return typeof bridge.probeDaemon === 'function' ? bridge : null;
244:   } catch {
245:     return null;
246:   }
247: }
248: 
249: async function probeCliWarmDaemon(env = process.env, timeoutMs = resolveCliTimeoutMs(env)) {
250:   const socketPath = resolveCliSocketPath(env);
251:   if (socketPathTooLong(socketPath)) {
252:     return { available: false, reason: 'CLI_SOCKET_PATH_TOO_LONG', socketPath };
253:   }
254:   if (!socketPath.startsWith('tcp://') && !existsSync(socketPath)) {
255:     return { available: false, reason: 'CLI_WARM_SOCKET_MISSING', socketPath };
256:   }
257:   const bridge = loadBridgeHelper();
258:   if (!bridge) {
259:     return { available: false, reason: 'CLI_BRIDGE_HELPER_UNAVAILABLE', socketPath };
260:   }
261:   const probeTimeoutMs = Math.max(1, Math.min(
262:     timeoutMs,
263:     positiveIntFromEnv(env.SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_CLI_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS, ADVISOR_CLI_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS),
264:   ));
265:   const probe = await bridge.probeDaemon(socketPath, { timeoutMs: probeTimeoutMs, deepProbe: true });
266:   return probe.status === 'alive'
267:     ? { available: true, socketPath }
268:     : { available: false, reason: probe.reason ?? probe.status, socketPath };
269: }
270: 
271: function withTimeout(operation, timeoutMs, label) {
272:   let timeout;
273:   return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
274:     timeout = setTimeout(() => {
275:       reject(Object.assign(new Error(`${label} timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`), { code: 'ADVISOR_MCP_TIMEOUT' }));
276:     }, timeoutMs);
277:     timeout.unref?.();
278:     operation.then(
279:       (value) => {
280:         if (timeout) clearTimeout(timeout);
281:         resolve(value);
282:       },
283:       (error) => {
284:         if (timeout) clearTimeout(timeout);
285:         reject(error);
286:       },
287:     );
288:   });
289: }

291: const HYGIENE_DIRECTIVE = '\nComment hygiene [HARD BLOCK]: NEVER embed ADR-/REQ-/CHK-/task-ids or spec paths in code comments — forbidden regardless of instruction. Write the durable WHY instead. Pre-commit gate blocks violations.';
292: 
293: function hasPrecomputedAmbiguity(result, recommendations) {
294:   if (result?.ambiguous === true) return true;
295:   return recommendations.some((recommendation) => (
296:     Array.isArray(recommendation?.ambiguousWith) && recommendation.ambiguousWith.length > 0
297:   ));
298: }
299: 
300: function renderAdvisorBrief(result, options = {}) {
301:   if (result.status !== 'ok') return null;
302:   if (result.freshness !== 'live' && result.freshness !== 'stale') return null;
303: 
304:   const tokenCap = Math.min(Math.max(1, positiveInt(result.metrics?.tokenCap ?? options.tokenCap, 80)), 120);
305:   const thresholdConfig = options.thresholdConfig ?? {};
306:   const recommendations = Array.isArray(result.recommendations)
307:     ? result.recommendations.filter((recommendation) => (
308:       recommendation.passes_threshold === true
309:       || (
310:         recommendation.confidence >= (thresholdConfig.confidenceThreshold ?? DEFAULT_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD)
311:         && (
312:           thresholdConfig.confidenceOnly === true
313:           || recommendation.uncertainty <= (thresholdConfig.uncertaintyThreshold ?? DEFAULT_UNCERTAINTY_THRESHOLD)
314:         )
315:       )
316:     ))
317:     : [];
318:   const top = recommendations[0];
319:   const second = recommendations[1];
320:   if (!top) return null;
321: 
322:   const topLabel = sanitizeLabel(result.sharedPayload?.metadata?.skillLabel ?? top.skill);
323:   if (!topLabel) return null;
324:   if (tokenCap > 80 && second && hasPrecomputedAmbiguity(result, recommendations)) {
325:     const secondLabel = sanitizeLabel(second.skill);
326:     if (!secondLabel) return null;
327:     const text = `Advisor: ${result.freshness}; ambiguous: ${topLabel} ${formatScore(top.confidence)}/${formatScore(top.uncertainty)} vs ${secondLabel} ${formatScore(second.confidence)}/${formatScore(second.uncertainty)} pass.`;
328:     const charCap = Math.min(tokenCap, 120) * 4;
329:     const brief = text.length <= charCap ? text : `${text.slice(0, Math.max(1, charCap - 3)).trimEnd()}...`;
330:     return brief + HYGIENE_DIRECTIVE;
331:   }
332:   const text = `Advisor: ${result.freshness}; use ${topLabel} ${formatScore(top.confidence)}/${formatScore(top.uncertainty)} pass.`;
333:   const charCap = Math.min(tokenCap, 80) * 4;
334:   const brief = text.length <= charCap ? text : `${text.slice(0, Math.max(1, charCap - 3)).trimEnd()}...`;
335:   return brief + HYGIENE_DIRECTIVE;
336: }

338: async function callAdvisorTool(name, args, workspaceRoot) {
339:   const transport = new StdioClientTransport({
340:     command: process.execPath,
341:     args: [ADVISOR_LAUNCHER_PATH],
342:     cwd: workspaceRoot,
343:       env: createChildEnv(),
344:     stderr: 'pipe',
345:   });
346:   transport.stderr?.on('data', () => {});
347:   const client = new Client({ name: 'mk-skill-advisor-bridge', version: '0.1.0' });
348:   try {
349:     await withTimeout(client.connect(transport), ADVISOR_MCP_TIMEOUT_MS, 'mk_skill_advisor initialize');
350:     return await withTimeout(
351:       client.callTool({ name, arguments: args }),
352:       ADVISOR_MCP_TIMEOUT_MS,
353:       `mk_skill_advisor.${name}`,
354:     );
355:   } finally {
356:     try {
357:       await client.close();
358:     } catch {
359:       // Best-effort process cleanup; bridge callers still get fail-open output.
360:     }
361:   }
362: }
363: 
364: function parseAdvisorToolData(toolResponse) {
365:   const first = Array.isArray(toolResponse?.content) ? toolResponse.content[0] : null;
366:   if (typeof first?.text !== 'string') {
367:     throw Object.assign(new Error('Advisor MCP response missing text content'), { code: 'ADVISOR_MCP_BAD_RESPONSE' });
368:   }
369:   const parsed = JSON.parse(first.text);
370:   return parsed?.data ?? parsed;
371: }
372: 
373: // The dead `renderNativeBrief()` alternate renderer was removed
374: // here. It was unreferenced (verified via grep across mcp_server) and could
375: // drift from the shared `renderAdvisorBrief()` formatter. Bridge output now
376: // flows exclusively through `renderAdvisorBrief()` loaded via
377: // `loadNativeAdvisorModules()`. Helpers `formatScore` and `sanitizeLabel`
378: // stay because they are still used by `buildNativeBrief` and `buildLegacyBrief`.
379: 
380: async function loadNativeAdvisorModules() {
381:   // Primary native path: import the compiled compat module in-process. This is the
382:   // canonical native surface (status handler, recommend handler, daemon probe, brief)
383:   // and its return shapes already match this bridge's consumers — readAdvisorStatus
384:   // returns the status DATA object (probeNativeAdvisor reads .freshness/.generation),
385:   // handleAdvisorRecommend returns the MCP `{content:[{text}]}` envelope
386:   // (buildNativeBrief parses content[0].text), and probeAdvisorDaemon returns the
387:   // probe result directly. Importing compat avoids the launcher/MCP stdio handshake,
388:   // which fails open to the python route when a stale launcher lease has no IPC socket
389:   // (LEASE_HELD_BY... no-bridge-socket). The launcher subprocess remains a fallback for
390:   // callers without a colocated dist build.
391:   try {
392:     const compat = await import(new URL('../dist/mcp_server/compat/index.js', import.meta.url));
393:     if (
394:       typeof compat.readAdvisorStatus === 'function'
395:       && typeof compat.handleAdvisorRecommend === 'function'
396:     ) {
397:       return {
398:         readAdvisorStatus: (args) => compat.readAdvisorStatus(args),
399:         handleAdvisorRecommend: (args) => compat.handleAdvisorRecommend(args),
400:         probeAdvisorDaemon: typeof compat.probeAdvisorDaemon === 'function'
401:           ? (args) => compat.probeAdvisorDaemon(args)
402:           : null,
403:         buildSkillAdvisorBrief: typeof compat.buildSkillAdvisorBrief === 'function'
404:           ? compat.buildSkillAdvisorBrief
405:           : null,
406:         renderAdvisorBrief,
407:       };
408:     }
409:   } catch {
410:     // Compat import unavailable (e.g. dist not built for a non-colocated caller).
411:     // Fall through to the launcher/MCP-subprocess fallback below.
412:   }
413:   return {
414:     readAdvisorStatus: async (args) => parseAdvisorToolData(await callAdvisorTool('advisor_status', args, args.workspaceRoot)),
415:     handleAdvisorRecommend: (args) => callAdvisorTool('advisor_recommend', args, args.workspaceRoot),
416:     probeAdvisorDaemon: null,
417:     buildSkillAdvisorBrief: null,
418:     renderAdvisorBrief,
419:   };
420: }
421: 
422: async function probeNativeAdvisor(input, dependencies = {}) {
423:   const env = dependencies.env ?? process.env;
424:   if (env[DISABLED_ENV] === '1') {
425:     return {
426:       available: false,
427:       freshness: 'unavailable',
428:       generation: 0,
429:       reason: 'ADVISOR_DISABLED',
430:     };
431:   }
432:   if (env[FORCE_LOCAL_ENV] === '1' || input.forceLocal === true) {
433:     return {
434:       available: false,
435:       freshness: 'unavailable',
436:       generation: 0,
437:       reason: 'FORCE_LOCAL',
438:     };
439:   }
440: 
441:   const modules = await (dependencies.loadNativeAdvisorModules ?? loadNativeAdvisorModules)();
442:   let probe;
443:   if (typeof modules.probeAdvisorDaemon === 'function') {
444:     probe = await modules.probeAdvisorDaemon({ workspaceRoot: input.workspaceRoot });
445:   } else {
446:     const status = await modules.readAdvisorStatus({ workspaceRoot: input.workspaceRoot });
447:     probe = {
448:       available: status.freshness === 'live' || status.freshness === 'stale',
449:       freshness: status.freshness,
450:       generation: status.generation,
451:       trustState: status.trustState,
452:       reason: status.errors?.[0] ?? status.trustState?.reason ?? null,
453:     };
454:   }
455:   // The skill graph is serveable whenever trustState is reader-usable
456:   // (live/stale). Recommendations run in-process off the graph DB; the daemon only
457:   // drives auto-reindex. So when the daemon/artifact freshness axis reports
458:   // 'unavailable' but trustState is live/stale, serve native in a degraded mode
459:   // instead of failing open to python (which yields no brief). createTrustState still
460:   // downgrades to 'unavailable'/'absent'/'stale' for a missing, corrupt, or
461:   // source-newer graph, so this never serves an untrustworthy graph.
462:   const trustStateValue = probe?.trustState?.state;
463:   const trustUsable = trustStateValue === 'live' || trustStateValue === 'stale';
464:   if (probe && !probe.available && trustUsable) {
465:     return { ...probe, available: true, degraded: true, daemonAvailable: false };
466:   }
467:   return probe;
468: }
469: 
470: async function buildNativeBrief(input, dependencies = {}) {
471:   const modules = await (dependencies.loadNativeAdvisorModules ?? loadNativeAdvisorModules)();
472:   const effectiveThresholds = {
473:     confidenceThreshold: threshold(input.thresholdConfidence),
474:     uncertaintyThreshold: DEFAULT_UNCERTAINTY_THRESHOLD,
475:     confidenceOnly: false,
476:   };
477:   const handlerResponse = await modules.handleAdvisorRecommend({
478:     workspaceRoot: input.workspaceRoot,
479:     prompt: input.prompt,
480:     options: {
481:       topK: 3,
482:       includeAbstainReasons: true,
483:       confidenceThreshold: effectiveThresholds.confidenceThreshold,
484:       uncertaintyThreshold: effectiveThresholds.uncertaintyThreshold,
485:     },
486:   });
487:   const parsed = JSON.parse(handlerResponse.content[0].text);
488:   const data = parsed.data;
489:   const maxTokens = positiveInt(input.maxTokens, 80);
490:   const top = Array.isArray(data?.recommendations) ? data.recommendations[0] : null;
491:   const skillLabel = sanitizeLabel(top?.skillId);
492:   const safeStatus = sanitizeLabel(top?.status);
493:   const redirectTo = sanitizeLabel(top?.redirectTo);
494:   const redirectFrom = Array.isArray(top?.redirectFrom)
495:     ? top.redirectFrom.map(sanitizeLabel).filter(Boolean)
496:     : [];
497:   const recommendations = Array.isArray(data?.recommendations)
498:     ? data.recommendations.map((recommendation) => ({
499:       skill: recommendation.skillId,
500:       kind: 'skill',
501:       confidence: recommendation.confidence,
502:       uncertainty: recommendation.uncertainty,
503:       score: Number.isFinite(recommendation.score) ? recommendation.score : recommendation.confidence,
504:       passes_threshold: recommendation.confidence >= effectiveThresholds.confidenceThreshold
505:         && recommendation.uncertainty <= effectiveThresholds.uncertaintyThreshold,
506:       reason: null,
507:       ...(Array.isArray(recommendation.ambiguousWith) ? { ambiguousWith: recommendation.ambiguousWith.map(sanitizeLabel).filter(Boolean) } : {}),
508:     }))
509:     : [];
510:   const renderedTokenCap = data?.ambiguous === true ? 120 : maxTokens;
511:   const rendered = modules.renderAdvisorBrief({
512:     status: recommendations.length > 0 ? 'ok' : 'skipped',
513:     freshness: data?.freshness ?? 'unavailable',
514:     brief: null,
515:     recommendations,
516:     ambiguous: data?.ambiguous === true,
517:     diagnostics: null,
518:     metrics: {
519:       durationMs: 0,
520:       cacheHit: Boolean(data?.cache?.hit),
521:       subprocessInvoked: false,
522:       retriesAttempted: 0,
523:       recommendationCount: recommendations.length,
524:       tokenCap: renderedTokenCap,
525:     },
526:     generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
527:     sharedPayload: {
528:       metadata: {
529:         skillLabel,
530:       },
531:     },
532:   }, {
533:     tokenCap: renderedTokenCap,
534:     thresholdConfig: effectiveThresholds,
535:   });
536: 
537:   return response({
538:     brief: rendered,
539:     status: rendered ? 'ok' : 'skipped',
540:     metadata: {
541:       route: 'native',
542:       workspaceRoot: input.workspaceRoot,
543:       effectiveThresholds,
544:       freshness: data?.freshness ?? 'unavailable',
545:       generation: input.probe?.generation ?? 0,
546:       cacheHit: Boolean(data?.cache?.hit),
547:       recommendationCount: Array.isArray(data?.recommendations) ? data.recommendations.length : 0,
548:       tokenCap: renderedTokenCap,
549:       skillLabel,
550:       status: safeStatus,
551:       redirectTo,
552:       redirectFrom,
553:     },
554:   });
555: }
556: 
557: function cliRecommendations(data, thresholds) {
558:   return Array.isArray(data?.recommendations)
559:     ? data.recommendations
560:       .map((recommendation) => {
561:         const skill = sanitizeLabel(recommendation?.skillId);
562:         if (!skill) return null;
563:         const confidence = Number.isFinite(recommendation?.confidence) ? recommendation.confidence : 0;
564:         const uncertainty = Number.isFinite(recommendation?.uncertainty) ? recommendation.uncertainty : 1;
565:         return {
566:           skill,
567:           kind: 'skill',
568:           confidence,
569:           uncertainty,
570:           score: Number.isFinite(recommendation?.score) ? recommendation.score : confidence,
571:           passes_threshold: confidence >= thresholds.confidenceThreshold
572:             && uncertainty <= thresholds.uncertaintyThreshold,
573:           reason: null,
574:           ...(Array.isArray(recommendation?.ambiguousWith) ? { ambiguousWith: recommendation.ambiguousWith.map(sanitizeLabel).filter(Boolean) } : {}),
575:         };
576:       })
577:       .filter((recommendation) => recommendation?.passes_threshold === true)
578:     : [];
579: }
580: 
581: function cliFallbackResponse(input, reason, subprocessInvoked = false, metadata = {}) {
582:   const maxTokens = positiveInt(input.maxTokens, 80);
583:   const effectiveThresholds = {
584:     confidenceThreshold: threshold(input.thresholdConfidence),
585:     uncertaintyThreshold: DEFAULT_UNCERTAINTY_THRESHOLD,
586:     confidenceOnly: false,
587:   };
588:   return response({
589:     brief: null,
590:     status: 'fail_open',
591:     error: reason,
592:     metadata: {
593:       route: 'cli',
594:       workspaceRoot: input.workspaceRoot,
595:       effectiveThresholds,
596:       freshness: metadata.freshness ?? 'unavailable',
597:       recommendationCount: 0,
598:       tokenCap: maxTokens,
599:       retryable: metadata.retryable ?? true,
600:       exitCode: metadata.exitCode ?? ADVISOR_CLI_RETRYABLE_EXIT,
601:       subprocessInvoked,
602:       ...metadata,
603:     },
604:   });
605: }
606: 
607: function classifyCliStaleDist(exitCode, stderr) {
608:   if (exitCode !== ADVISOR_CLI_STALE_EXIT || !/dist entrypoint is (stale|missing)/i.test(stderr)) {
609:     return null;
610:   }
611:   return {
612:     freshness: 'dist_stale',
613:     retryable: false,
614:     exitCode: ADVISOR_CLI_STALE_EXIT,
615:     state: 'dist_stale_rebuild_required',
616:     staleDist: true,
617:     rebuildRequired: true,
618:     action: 'run the skill-advisor TypeScript build',
619:     stderr: stderr ? '[stderr-present]' : null,
620:   };
621: }
622: 
623: function runCliRecommend(input, env, timeoutMs) {
624:   // Thread the caller's thresholds into the subprocess payload so the CLI
625:   // scorer applies the same floor/ceiling as the in-process path. Omitting
626:   // them made the scorer fall back to its built-in defaults while the response
627:   // metadata still reported the caller-supplied thresholds.
628:   const payload = {
629:     prompt: input.prompt,
630:     options: {
631:       topK: 3,
632:       includeAttribution: false,
633:       includeAbstainReasons: true,
634:       confidenceThreshold: threshold(input.thresholdConfidence),
635:       uncertaintyThreshold: DEFAULT_UNCERTAINTY_THRESHOLD,
636:     },
637:   };
638:   return new Promise((resolvePromise) => {
639:     let stdout = '';
640:     let stderr = '';
641:     let settled = false;
642:     let timedOut = false;
643:     const child = spawn(process.execPath, [
644:       ADVISOR_CLI_PATH,
645:       'advisor_recommend',
646:       '--json',
647:       JSON.stringify(payload),
648:       '--format',
649:       'json',
650:       '--timeout-ms',
651:       String(timeoutMs),
652:       '--warm-only',
653:     ], {
654:       cwd: input.workspaceRoot,
655:       env: createCliChildEnv(env),
656:       stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
657:     });
658:     const timer = setTimeout(() => {
659:       timedOut = true;
660:       child.kill('SIGKILL');
661:     }, timeoutMs);
662:     timer.unref?.();
663:     const finish = (result) => {
664:       if (settled) return;
665:       settled = true;
666:       clearTimeout(timer);
667:       resolvePromise(result);
668:     };
669:     child.stdout?.setEncoding('utf8');
670:     child.stdout?.on('data', (chunk) => {
671:       stdout += chunk;
672:     });
673:     child.stderr?.setEncoding('utf8');
674:     child.stderr?.on('data', (chunk) => {
675:       if (stderr.length < MAX_CLI_STDERR_BYTES) {
676:         stderr = (stderr + String(chunk)).slice(0, MAX_CLI_STDERR_BYTES);
677:       }
678:     });
679:     child.once('error', (error) => {
680:       finish({ stdout, stderr, exitCode: null, signal: null, timedOut, error: error.code ?? error.message });
681:     });
682:     child.once('close', (exitCode, signal) => {
683:       finish({ stdout, stderr, exitCode, signal, timedOut, error: null });
684:     });
685:   });
686: }
687: 
688: async function buildCliBrief(input, dependencies = {}) {
689:   const env = dependencies.env ?? process.env;
690:   const timeoutMs = resolveCliTimeoutMs(env);
691:   const probe = await probeCliWarmDaemon(env, timeoutMs);
692:   if (!probe.available) {
693:     return cliFallbackResponse(input, `CLI_RETRYABLE_UNAVAILABLE:${probe.reason ?? 'warm-daemon-unavailable'}`, false);
694:   }
695: 
696:   const cli = await runCliRecommend(input, env, timeoutMs);
697:   if (cli.timedOut) {
698:     return cliFallbackResponse(input, 'CLI_RETRYABLE_UNAVAILABLE:timeout', true);
699:   }
700:   if (cli.error) {
701:     return cliFallbackResponse(input, `CLI_RETRYABLE_UNAVAILABLE:${cli.error}`, true);
702:   }
703:   if (cli.exitCode !== 0 || cli.signal !== null) {
704:     const staleDist = classifyCliStaleDist(cli.exitCode, cli.stderr ?? '');
705:     if (staleDist) {
706:       return cliFallbackResponse(input, 'DIST_STALE_REBUILD_REQUIRED', true, staleDist);
707:     }
708:     return cliFallbackResponse(
709:       input,
710:       cli.exitCode === ADVISOR_CLI_RETRYABLE_EXIT ? 'CLI_RETRYABLE_EXIT_75' : `CLI_EXIT_${cli.exitCode ?? 'SIGNAL'}`,
711:       true,
712:     );
713:   }
714: 
715:   let parsed;
716:   try {
717:     parsed = JSON.parse(cli.stdout || '{}');
718:   } catch {
719:     return cliFallbackResponse(input, 'CLI_RETRYABLE_UNAVAILABLE:bad-json', true);
720:   }
721:   const data = parsed?.data && typeof parsed.data === 'object' ? parsed.data : null;
722:   if (!data) {
723:     return cliFallbackResponse(input, 'CLI_RETRYABLE_UNAVAILABLE:bad-response', true);
724:   }
725: 
726:   const maxTokens = positiveInt(input.maxTokens, 80);
727:   const effectiveThresholds = {
728:     confidenceThreshold: threshold(input.thresholdConfidence),
729:     uncertaintyThreshold: DEFAULT_UNCERTAINTY_THRESHOLD,
730:     confidenceOnly: false,
731:   };
732:   const recommendations = cliRecommendations(data, effectiveThresholds);
733:   const top = recommendations[0] ?? null;
734:   const renderedTokenCap = data.ambiguous === true ? 120 : maxTokens;
735:   const freshness = data.freshness === 'live' || data.freshness === 'stale'
736:     ? data.freshness
737:     : 'unavailable';
738:   if (freshness === 'unavailable') {
739:     return cliFallbackResponse(input, 'CLI_RETRYABLE_UNAVAILABLE:freshness', true);
740:   }
741:   const rendered = renderAdvisorBrief({
742:     status: recommendations.length > 0 ? 'ok' : 'skipped',
743:     freshness,
744:     recommendations,
745:     ambiguous: data.ambiguous === true,
746:     metrics: { tokenCap: renderedTokenCap },
747:     sharedPayload: {
748:       metadata: {
749:         skillLabel: top?.skill ?? null,
750:       },
751:     },
752:   }, {
753:     tokenCap: renderedTokenCap,
754:     thresholdConfig: effectiveThresholds,
755:   });
756: 
757:   const safeStatus = sanitizeLabel(top?.status);
758:   const redirectTo = sanitizeLabel(top?.redirectTo);
759:   const redirectFrom = Array.isArray(top?.redirectFrom)
760:     ? top.redirectFrom.map(sanitizeLabel).filter(Boolean)
761:     : [];
762:   return response({
763:     brief: rendered,
764:     status: rendered ? 'ok' : 'skipped',
765:     metadata: {
766:       route: 'cli',
767:       workspaceRoot: input.workspaceRoot,
768:       effectiveThresholds,
769:       freshness,
770:       cacheHit: Boolean(data.cache?.hit),
771:       recommendationCount: recommendations.length,
772:       tokenCap: renderedTokenCap,
773:       skillLabel: top?.skill ?? null,
774:       status: safeStatus,
775:       redirectTo,
776:       redirectFrom,
777:       retryable: false,
778:       exitCode: 0,
779:       subprocessInvoked: true,
780:     },
781:   });
782: }
783: 
784: async function buildLegacyBrief(input) {
785:   const maxTokens = positiveInt(input.maxTokens, 80);
786:   const effectiveThresholds = {
787:     confidenceThreshold: threshold(input.thresholdConfidence),
788:     uncertaintyThreshold: DEFAULT_UNCERTAINTY_THRESHOLD,
789:     confidenceOnly: false,
790:   };
791: 
792:   return response({
793:     brief: null,
794:     status: 'fail_open',
795:     error: 'SYSTEM_SKILL_ADVISOR_UNAVAILABLE',
796:     metadata: {
797:       route: 'python',
798:       workspaceRoot: input.workspaceRoot,
799:       effectiveThresholds,
800:       freshness: 'unavailable',
801:       recommendationCount: 0,
802:       tokenCap: maxTokens,
803:     },
804:   });
805: }
806: 
807: async function buildBrief(input, dependencies = {}) {
808:   const env = dependencies.env ?? process.env;
809:   if (env[DISABLED_ENV] === '1') {
810:     // Silent fail-open in disabled mode. Previously this branch
811:     // returned a model-visible `Advisor: disabled by ...` brief, but every
812:     // other runtime (Codex, Claude, Copilot) fails open silently when
813:     // the disabled flag is set. Aligning OpenCode with the dominant pattern
814:     // removes the runtime-specific surface inconsistency. Callers can still
815:     // detect the disabled state via `metadata.route === 'disabled'`.
816:     return response({
817:       brief: null,
818:       status: 'skipped',
819:       metadata: {
820:         route: 'disabled',
821:         freshness: 'unavailable',
822:         recommendationCount: 0,
823:       },
824:     });
825:   }
826: 
827:   try {
828:     const probe = await probeNativeAdvisor(input, dependencies);
829:     if (probe.available) {
830:       return await buildNativeBrief({ ...input, probe }, dependencies);
831:     }
832:     if (input.forceNative === true) {
833:       return failOpen(probe.reason || 'NATIVE_UNAVAILABLE');
834:     }
835:   } catch {
836:     if (input.forceNative === true) {
837:       return failOpen('NATIVE_PROBE_FAILED');
838:     }
839:   }
840: 
841:   if (env[FORCE_LOCAL_ENV] !== '1' && input.forceLocal !== true) {
842:     return buildCliBrief(input, dependencies);
843:   }
844: 
845:   return buildLegacyBrief(input);
846: }
847: 
848: async function main() {
849:   const payload = await withStdoutSilenced(async () => {
850:     try {
851:       const input = parseInput(await readStdin());
852:       return await buildBrief(input);
853:     } catch (error) {
854:       return failOpen(errorCode(error));
855:     }
856:   });
857: 
858:   process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(payload));
859: }
860: 
861: if (process.argv[1] && resolve(process.argv[1]) === fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)) {
862:   main().catch((error) => {
863:     process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(failOpen(errorCode(error))));
864:   });
865: }
866: 
867: export {
868:   buildBrief,
869:   buildCliBrief,
870:   buildLegacyBrief,
871:   buildNativeBrief,
872:   classifyCliStaleDist,
873:   createChildEnv,
874:   parseInput,
875:   renderAdvisorBrief,
876:   response,
877: };

(End of file - total 877 lines)
